dependencies.relationship: Uses store layer, config, database schema, Echo server, and health probe infrastructure from spec 01

task_groups.title: Write tests: OAuth provider registry and OAuth endpoints

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for provider interface and GitHub provider defaults
details: Create internal/auth/provider_test.go
Test that the Provider interface declares AuthorizeURL, ExchangeCode, and GetUserInfo methods
Test GitHub provider built-in defaults: authorize_url, token_url, userinfo_url
Test that config.toml overrides take precedence over built-in defaults
Test that a mock provider implementing the interface can be registered and retrieved without changes to middleware or handlers

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for provider removal and OAuth callback provider listing
details: Create internal/integration/oauth_provider_test.go
Test that removing a provider from config retains existing user records and API keys in DB
Test that POST /api/v1/auth/callback with removed provider returns HTTP 400 with unsupported provider envelope
Test GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200 array with name and authorize_url fields, no secrets or credentials

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for OAuth callback user upsert and blocked user handling
details: Create internal/integration/oauth_callback_test.go
Set up mock HTTP server for GitHub token_url and userinfo_url
Test POST /api/v1/auth/callback creates new user with status=active and returns user object
Test POST /api/v1/auth/callback for existing blocked user returns HTTP 200 but does not change status to active
Verify DB state after each call

subtasks.title: Write edge case tests for OAuth callback error conditions
details: Test unknown provider name returns HTTP 400 with unsupported provider error envelope
Test identity provider timeout (mock token_url to time out) returns HTTP 500 with no user record written
Test missing required fields in POST body returns HTTP 400
Test expired/rejected authorization code returns HTTP 400, no user record created
Test oversized request body returns HTTP 413

checks: All test files in internal/auth/ and internal/integration/ compile without errors: go build ./...
Spec tests for this group pass (currently expected to fail — stubs not yet implemented): go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'Provider|OAuth|Callback'
No linter warnings introduced: go vet ./...
Requirements 02-REQ-1.1, 02-REQ-1.2, 02-REQ-1.3, 02-REQ-1.4, 02-REQ-2.1, 02-REQ-2.2, 02-REQ-2.3 test cases are present

task_groups.title: Write tests: Auth middleware and RBAC enforcement

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for auth middleware token validation
details: Create internal/integration/middleware_test.go
Test middleware applies to /api/v1/* but not /api/v1/auth/* or health routes
Test admin token (af_admin_...) SHA-256 hash is matched against admin_tokens; context populated with admin auth method
Test API key token (af_<key_id>_<secret>) parsed and verified; context populated with workspace_id and role
Test blocked user with valid token returns HTTP 403 with 'user is blocked' envelope; handler never invoked

subtasks.title: Write edge case tests for auth middleware rejection conditions
details: Test missing/malformed Authorization header returns HTTP 401 with 'missing or malformed token'
Test admin token hash mismatch returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid token'
Test API key not found, hash mismatch, revoked, and expired all return HTTP 401
Test DB error during token lookup returns HTTP 500 without leaking internal error details

subtasks.title: Write unit and integration tests for RBAC role enforcement
details: Create internal/auth/rbac_test.go
Test admin, editor, reader role constants are defined
Test non-admin attempting admin-only endpoint receives HTTP 403 before handler executes
Test non-admin user can update own full_name but receives HTTP 403 when attempting to change status
Test admin token bypasses all workspace scoping and can access all endpoints
Test editor API key permitted on write endpoints; reader API key rejected with HTTP 403

subtasks.title: Write edge case test for unknown role in request context
details: Create unit test in internal/auth/rbac_test.go for unknown role scenario
Manually populate request context with role='superuser'
Assert RBAC layer returns HTTP 403 with 'unknown role' error envelope

checks: All test files in internal/auth/ and internal/integration/ compile without errors: go build ./...
Spec tests for this group compile and are runnable (stubs expected): go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'Middleware|RBAC|Role'
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 02-REQ-3.1 through 02-REQ-3.4, 02-REQ-4.1 through 02-REQ-4.5 test cases are present and organized

task_groups.title: Write tests: User management, workspace management (part 1)

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for user management endpoints
details: Create internal/integration/user_handler_test.go
Test POST /api/v1/users creates user with status=active and returns HTTP 201
Test GET /api/v1/users returns all users as array with HTTP 200
Test GET /api/v1/users/:id returns user object with memberships array
Test PUT /api/v1/users/:id with admin credentials updates full_name and status, returns HTTP 200

subtasks.title: Write edge case tests for user management endpoints
details: Test POST /api/v1/users with duplicate username or (provider, provider_id) returns HTTP 409, no user created
Test GET and PUT on non-existent user ID return HTTP 404 with 'user not found'
Test POST /api/v1/users missing required fields returns HTTP 400

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for workspace creation and listing
details: Create internal/integration/workspace_handler_test.go
Test POST /api/v1/workspaces validates slug and URL, creates workspace, returns HTTP 201
Test GET /api/v1/workspaces excludes archived by default; includes archived when ?include_archived=true

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for workspace archive, reactivate, and delete
details: Test POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive marks active workspace as archived, returns HTTP 200
Test POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate marks archived workspace as active, returns HTTP 200
Test DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id on archived workspace cascades deletion of memberships and API keys, returns HTTP 200

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for workspace member management
details: Test POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members adds or updates membership with given role, returns HTTP 200
Test GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members returns array of all member records with user_id and role

checks: Test files compile without errors: go build ./...
User and workspace handler test files are present and runnable: go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'User|Workspace'
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 02-REQ-5.1 through 02-REQ-5.4, 02-REQ-6.1 through 02-REQ-6.7 test cases are covered

task_groups.title: Write tests: Workspace edge cases, API key management, error envelope, and documentation

subtasks.title: Write edge case tests for workspace management endpoints
details: Test duplicate name or slug returns HTTP 409 with workspace conflict envelope
Test invalid slug format or non-http/https URL returns HTTP 400
Test archiving already-archived workspace returns HTTP 400
Test reactivating non-archived workspace returns HTTP 400
Test deleting non-archived workspace returns HTTP 400, workspace intact
Test non-existent workspace ID on any endpoint returns HTTP 404
Test adding non-existent user_id as member returns HTTP 404
Test partial DB failure during cascade delete rolls back entire transaction; verify workspace/memberships/api_keys remain

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for API key management endpoints
details: Create internal/integration/apikey_handler_test.go
Test POST /api/v1/keys creates key in af_<key_id>_<secret> format, stores key_hash, sets expires_at=30d, returns plaintext once
Test GET /api/v1/keys with admin token returns all keys including expired; no plaintext secrets
Test GET /api/v1/keys with API key token returns only the authenticated user's keys
Test POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh generates new secret, updates key_hash, returns new key once
Test DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id sets revoked_at, returns HTTP 200 with revoked message

subtasks.title: Write edge case tests for API key management endpoints
details: Test creating key for workspace without membership returns HTTP 403 with 'not a member' envelope
Test invalid expires value (not 0/30/60/90) returns HTTP 400
Test non-existent workspace_id returns HTTP 404 when creating key
Test refresh or revoke on non-existent or other-user key returns HTTP 404
Test reader-role user attempting POST /api/v1/keys or DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id returns HTTP 403

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for standardized error response envelope and documentation
details: Create internal/handler/error_handler_test.go
Test all API error responses use {"error": {"code": "<STATUS>", "message": "..."}} format with correct HTTP status
Test all seven status codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 413, 500) are mapped correctly
Test 500-level responses never expose internal error details, stack traces, or DB error strings
Test that docs/api.md exists and contains all endpoint methods, paths, auth requirements, examples, and error format
Test that README.md contains a link to docs/api.md

checks: All new test files compile: go build ./...
Workspace edge case, API key, error envelope, and doc tests are runnable: go test ./internal/integration/... ./internal/handler/... -count=1 -v -run 'Workspace|APIKey|Error|Doc'
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 02-REQ-6.E1 through 02-REQ-6.E8, 02-REQ-7.1 through 02-REQ-7.E5, 02-REQ-8.1 through 02-REQ-9.2 test cases all present

subtasks.title: Write property tests for security invariants (admin token, API key secret, blocked user)
details: Create internal/auth/properties_test.go
TS-02-P1: For arbitrary admin tokens, assert SHA-256 hashes only stored; plaintext never in DB or responses
TS-02-P2: For any key create/refresh, assert plaintext returned exactly once; key_hash = sha256(secret) in DB; subsequent GETs show no plaintext
TS-02-P3: For any request from blocked user with valid admin or API key token, assert HTTP 403 always returned; handler never invoked

subtasks.title: Write property tests for workspace atomicity, blocked-user upsert, slug uniqueness, and error envelope
details: TS-02-P4: For DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id with simulated partial DB failure, assert either full success (all gone) or full rollback (all intact)
TS-02-P5: For OAuth callback with blocked user, assert status never changes from blocked to active in DB
TS-02-P6: For workspace creation with conflicting name or slug, assert HTTP 409 and no record created; unique creation returns HTTP 201
TS-02-P7: For any 5xx error, assert response body only contains standard envelope with 'internal server error'; no internal details leaked

subtasks.title: Write smoke tests covering all six execution paths
details: Create internal/integration/smoke_test.go
SMOKE-1: OAuth login for new user — discover providers, exchange code, verify DB user record
SMOKE-2: Admin creates workspace and assigns member — verify DB workspace and membership
SMOKE-3: Editor creates API key and uses it — verify plaintext returned once, DB key_hash correct, subsequent auth works
SMOKE-4: Blocked user with valid key rejected at middleware — verify HTTP 403, handler never invoked
SMOKE-5: Admin archives then deletes workspace — verify atomic cascade deletion of memberships and API keys
SMOKE-6: Non-admin updates own full_name — verify HTTP 200, status unchanged

checks: Property test and smoke test files compile: go build ./...
Test file structure is correct: go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'Property|Smoke'
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
All property IDs (02-PROP-1 through 02-PROP-7) and execution paths (02-PATH-1 through 02-PATH-6) have corresponding test coverage

task_groups.title: Implement OAuth provider registry and GitHub provider

subtasks.title: Define Provider interface and registry type
details: Create internal/auth/provider.go

Define Provider interface with methods: AuthorizeURL(redirectURI string) string, ExchangeCode(ctx context.Context, code string, redirectURI string) (*TokenResponse, error), GetUserInfo(ctx context.Context, accessToken string) (*UserInfo, error)
Define Registry struct with Register(name string, p Provider) and GetProvider(name string) (Provider, error) methods
Define TokenResponse and UserInfo struct types

subtasks.title: Implement GitHub OAuth provider with config override support
details: Create internal/auth/github_provider.go

Implement Provider interface for GitHub with built-in defaults: authorize_url='https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize', token_url='https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token', userinfo_url='https://api.github.com/user'
Accept config overrides for all three URLs
Use standard net/http client for token exchange and user info retrieval
Parse GitHub userinfo response to extract username, email, and provider_id fields

subtasks.title: Initialize registry from config and wire into application startup
details: Create internal/auth/registry.go with NewRegistry(cfg Config) (*Registry, error)
Read provider list from config.toml; register only configured providers
Configure HTTP client with timeout from config (default 10s) for identity provider calls
Wire Registry into main application startup via dependency injection
Existing user records and API keys are unaffected when provider removed from config

checks: Provider interface and GitHub implementation compile: go build ./internal/auth/...
Unit tests for provider interface and GitHub defaults pass: go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -v -run 'Provider|GitHub|Registry'
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/auth/...
Requirements 02-REQ-1.1, 02-REQ-1.2, 02-REQ-1.3, 02-REQ-1.4 acceptance criteria met per TS-02-1, TS-02-2, TS-02-3

task_groups.title: Implement OAuth endpoints and auth handlers

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/auth/providers handler
details: Create internal/handler/auth_handler.go
Implement ListProviders(c echo.Context) error handler
Return array of {name, authorize_url} objects from registry; never expose client_secret, token_url, or credentials
Register route: GET /api/v1/auth/providers (excluded from auth middleware)

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/callback handler with user upsert
details: Implement OAuthCallback(c echo.Context) error handler
Parse and validate request body: provider, code, redirect_uri are required
Look up provider in registry; return HTTP 400 if not found
Exchange code via provider.ExchangeCode; retrieve user info via provider.GetUserInfo
Upsert user by (provider, provider_id): create with status=active if new; update username and email if existing; never change status from blocked to active
Return HTTP 200 with user object on success

subtasks.title: Implement standardized error response envelope and error handler
details: Create internal/handler/errors.go
Define ErrorResponse struct: {error: {code: string, message: string}}
Implement newErrorResponse(status int, message string) helper
Register Echo custom error handler that maps all errors to the standard envelope
For 5xx errors, always return generic 'internal server error' message; never expose stack traces, SQL text, or Go error strings
Set Content-Type: application/json on all error responses

checks: Auth handler and error handler compile: go build ./internal/handler/...
OAuth and error envelope integration tests pass: go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'OAuth|Callback|ErrorEnvelope'
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/handler/...
Requirements 02-REQ-2.1, 02-REQ-2.2, 02-REQ-2.3, 02-REQ-8.1, 02-REQ-8.2, 02-REQ-8.3 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement auth middleware and RBAC enforcement

subtasks.title: Implement auth middleware for admin tokens and API keys
details: Create internal/auth/middleware.go
Implement AuthMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc
Apply to /api/v1 group; exclude /api/v1/auth/* routes
Parse Authorization header: detect af_admin_... prefix vs af_<key_id>_<secret> format
For admin tokens: SHA-256 hash the token, query admin_tokens table, populate context with auth_method=admin, status=active
For API keys: extract key_id, query api_keys table, SHA-256 hash secret, compare key_hash; populate context with user_id, user_status, auth_method=api_key, workspace_id, role
Use context keys constants for user_id, role, workspace_id, auth_method, user_status

subtasks.title: Implement blocked user rejection and token error handling in middleware
details: After successful token verification, look up user status from DB
If status=blocked, return HTTP 403 with 'user is blocked' error envelope; do not call next handler
Return HTTP 401 for missing/malformed token, admin hash mismatch, API key not found, hash mismatch, revoked (revoked_at set), or expired (expires_at in past)
Return HTTP 500 without leaking error details when DB error occurs during token lookup
Ensure all error paths use the standard error envelope format

subtasks.title: Implement RBAC enforcement layer
details: Create internal/auth/rbac.go
Define role constants: RoleAdmin, RoleEditor, RoleReader
Implement RequireRole(roles ...string) echo.MiddlewareFunc for endpoint-level enforcement
Admin role bypasses all workspace scoping; apply globally
For PUT /api/v1/users/:id: allow non-admin to update own full_name only; reject status changes with HTTP 403
Reject unknown role values from context with HTTP 403 and 'unknown role' envelope
Editor permitted on write endpoints (POST /api/v1/keys, DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id); reader rejected with HTTP 403

checks: Auth middleware and RBAC compile: go build ./internal/auth/...
Middleware and RBAC integration tests pass: go test ./internal/integration/... ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -v -run 'Middleware|RBAC|Auth'
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/auth/...
Requirements 02-REQ-3.1 through 02-REQ-3.4, 02-REQ-4.1 through 02-REQ-4.5 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/users and GET /api/v1/users handlers
details: Create internal/handler/user_handler.go
Implement CreateUser(c echo.Context) error: parse body (username, email, provider, provider_id required), create user with status=active, return HTTP 201
Return HTTP 400 on missing required fields; HTTP 409 on duplicate username or (provider, provider_id)
Implement ListUsers(c echo.Context) error: return HTTP 200 with array of all user objects

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/users/:id and PUT /api/v1/users/:id handlers
details: Implement GetUser(c echo.Context) error: fetch user by ID including workspace memberships and roles; return HTTP 200 with user+memberships object or HTTP 404
Implement UpdateUser(c echo.Context) error: update full_name and/or status (active|blocked) with admin credentials; return HTTP 200 or HTTP 404
RBAC guard for status change: only admins may change status field; non-admin updating own record may only change full_name

subtasks.title: Register user management routes on the Echo group
details: Register routes in internal/handler/routes.go or equivalent wiring file
POST /api/v1/users → CreateUser (admin only via RBAC middleware)
GET /api/v1/users → ListUsers (admin only)
GET /api/v1/users/:id → GetUser (admin only)
PUT /api/v1/users/:id → UpdateUser (admin or self for full_name via RBAC)
Apply auth middleware and appropriate RBAC middleware to each route

checks: User handlers compile: go build ./internal/handler/...
User management integration tests pass: go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'User'
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/handler/...
Requirements 02-REQ-5.1 through 02-REQ-5.4 and edge cases 02-REQ-5.E1 through 02-REQ-5.E3 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement workspace creation and listing handlers
details: Create internal/handler/workspace_handler.go

Implement CreateWorkspace(c echo.Context) error: validate slug (regex: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$ or equivalent, 3-64 chars, starts with letter, no trailing hyphen) and URL (http/https scheme + host required)
Return HTTP 201 with workspace object on success; HTTP 400 for invalid slug/URL; HTTP 409 for duplicate name or slug
Implement ListWorkspaces(c echo.Context) error: exclude archived by default; include when ?include_archived=true is present; return HTTP 200

subtasks.title: Implement workspace archive, reactivate, and delete handlers
details: Implement ArchiveWorkspace(c echo.Context) error: mark as archived; HTTP 400 if already archived; HTTP 404 if not found
Implement ReactivateWorkspace(c echo.Context) error: mark as active; HTTP 400 if not archived; HTTP 404 if not found

Implement DeleteWorkspace(c echo.Context) error: require archived state (HTTP 400 if not archived); open DB transaction; delete memberships, delete api_keys, delete workspace; commit; return HTTP 200 with {"message": "workspace deleted"}
Roll back entire transaction on any DB error during cascade; return HTTP 500 without leaking details

subtasks.title: Implement workspace member management handlers and register all workspace routes
details: Implement AddOrUpdateMember(c echo.Context) error: upsert membership by (workspace_id, user_id); HTTP 404 if workspace or user not found; return HTTP 200 with membership object
Implement ListMembers(c echo.Context) error: return HTTP 200 with array of member objects; HTTP 404 if workspace not found

Register all workspace routes on admin-only group: POST, GET /api/v1/workspaces; POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive; POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate; DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id; POST, GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members

checks: Workspace handlers compile: go build ./internal/handler/...
Workspace integration tests pass: go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'Workspace'
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/handler/...
Requirements 02-REQ-6.1 through 02-REQ-6.7 and all edge cases met; atomicity property 02-PROP-4 verified by TS-02-P4 and TS-02-E21

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/keys handler for API key creation
details: Create internal/handler/apikey_handler.go
Implement CreateAPIKey(c echo.Context) error: validate workspace_id, label, expires (must be 0, 30, 60, or 90)
Verify user is a member of the workspace; return HTTP 403 if not; HTTP 404 if workspace not found
Generate key_id (UUID or random), secret (random bytes); construct af_<key_id>_<secret> token
Store SHA-256 hash of secret as key_hash; set expires_at = now + 24h×expires (null if expires=0); inherit user's role in workspace
Return HTTP 201 with plaintext key in response exactly once

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/keys, POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh, and DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id handlers
details: Implement ListAPIKeys(c echo.Context) error: admin token → return all keys across all users including expired; API key token → return only authenticated user's own keys; never include plaintext secrets in list
Implement RefreshAPIKey(c echo.Context) error: generate new secret for existing key_id; update key_hash; return new plaintext key once; HTTP 404 if key_id not found or belongs to another user
Implement RevokeAPIKey(c echo.Context) error: set revoked_at to current timestamp; return HTTP 200 with {"message": "key revoked"}; HTTP 404 if not found or belongs to another user
RBAC: reader role returns HTTP 403 on POST and DELETE key endpoints

subtasks.title: Register API key routes on the Echo group
details: Register routes on authenticated group: POST /api/v1/keys → CreateAPIKey (editor or admin via RBAC)
GET /api/v1/keys → ListAPIKeys (any authenticated user)
POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh → RefreshAPIKey (editor or admin via RBAC)
DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id → RevokeAPIKey (editor or admin via RBAC)
Apply auth middleware and RBAC role guards to each route per the permission matrix

checks: API key handlers compile: go build ./internal/handler/...
API key management integration tests pass: go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'APIKey|Key'
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/handler/...
Requirements 02-REQ-7.1 through 02-REQ-7.5 and edge cases 02-REQ-7.E1 through 02-REQ-7.E5 acceptance criteria met
Property TS-02-P2 (key secret returned exactly once) verified

subtasks.title: Audit error handling coverage across all handlers

Ensure all error responses use the standard {"error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}} envelope
Confirm 500-level responses never expose internal error details

checks: Full test suite passes with zero failures: go test ./... -count=1
No linter warnings across all packages: go vet ./...
All 37 regular test cases (TS-02-1 through TS-02-37), 7 property tests (TS-02-P1 through TS-02-P7), and 26 edge case tests (TS-02-E1 through TS-02-E26) pass
Zero skipped tests in the output

subtasks.title: Write docs/api.md with complete REST API documentation
details: Create docs/api.md

Document every endpoint: GET /api/v1/auth/providers, POST /api/v1/auth/callback, POST /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users/:id, PUT /api/v1/users/:id, POST /api/v1/workspaces, GET /api/v1/workspaces, POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive, POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate, DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id, POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members, GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members, POST /api/v1/keys, GET /api/v1/keys, POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh, DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id
For each endpoint: method, path, auth requirements (Bearer token type), JSON request body example, JSON response body example(s), all status codes, and error envelope format examples

subtasks.title: Update README.md with link to docs/api.md
details: Add a section to README.md: '## API Documentation' with a markdown link: [REST API Reference](docs/api.md)
Ensure the link is a relative path that works when browsing the repository on GitHub

Documentation tests pass: go test ./... -count=1 -v -run 'Doc|API'
README.md contains the string 'docs/api.md'
Requirements 02-REQ-9.1 and 02-REQ-9.2 acceptance criteria met per TS-02-36 and TS-02-37

task_groups.title: Wiring verification — end-to-end integration and stub audit

subtasks.title: Trace all six execution paths through production code
details: 02-PATH-1: Verify GET /api/v1/auth/providers → ListProviders → Registry.GetProvider chain is fully connected
02-PATH-2: Verify admin token → AuthMiddleware → RBAC → CreateWorkspace → DB store chain; POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members → AddOrUpdateMember chain
02-PATH-3: Verify API key token → AuthMiddleware → RBAC editor check → CreateAPIKey → key_hash stored in DB → new key usable for subsequent auth
02-PATH-4: Verify valid API key → AuthMiddleware → user status check → HTTP 403 returned before handler invoked
02-PATH-5: Verify ArchiveWorkspace → DB update; DeleteWorkspace → transaction wrapping memberships + api_keys + workspace deletion → DB commit
02-PATH-6: Verify reader API key → AuthMiddleware → RBAC self-update check → UpdateUser full_name only path

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across all handler → store → response chains
details: Confirm that (*User, error) from store.CreateUser is returned in HTTP 201 response body
Confirm that plaintext API key returned by CreateAPIKey handler matches af_<key_id>_<secret> format and is not retrievable via GET /api/v1/keys
Confirm that transaction error from DeleteWorkspace propagates to HTTP 500 with rollback; no partial state left in DB
Confirm that key_hash updated after RefreshAPIKey is actually persisted to DB and the old secret no longer authenticates

subtasks.title: Run all six smoke tests with real components
details: Execute all SMOKE tests against real Echo server + real DB (SQLite in test mode); only GitHub HTTP endpoints are mocked
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-1: providers endpoint returns github; callback creates user in DB
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-2: workspace created and membership record present in DB after both API calls
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-3: new key key_hash = sha256(secret) in DB; subsequent request with new key returns HTTP 200
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-4: blocked user's valid key returns HTTP 403; handler not invoked
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-5: workspace, memberships, api_keys all deleted after archive + delete sequence
Confirm TS-02-SMOKE-6: full_name updated in DB; status unchanged after reader self-update

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit across all auth and handler packages
details: Search for panic("not implemented") in all Go files: grep -r 'panic("not implemented")' internal/
Search for bare 'return nil' in non-trivial functions that should return (*Type, error): grep -n 'return nil$' internal/
Search for // TODO and // FIXME markers: grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME' internal/
Confirm zero stub markers remain in production code paths
Verify all Provider interface methods are fully implemented in GitHubProvider struct (no method stubs)

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point verification — confirm store layer calls are wired to spec 01 implementations
details: Confirm auth middleware calls store.GetAdminTokenByHash and store.GetAPIKeyByKeyID from spec 01 store layer (not stubs)
Confirm workspace handler calls store.CreateWorkspace, store.ArchiveWorkspace, store.DeleteWorkspaceTx from spec 01 store
